nl_question: For delivered orders, group by the year and month of order_purchase_timestamp and show the number of orders, the average actual delivery days, the average promised days, and the late rate. Only months with at least 500 orders, ordered chronologically.
sql: SELECT YEAR(TRY_TO_TIMESTAMP("order_purchase_timestamp")) AS order_year, MONTH(TRY_TO_TIMESTAMP("order_purchase_timestamp")) AS order_month, COUNT(*) AS num_orders, ROUND(AVG(DATEDIFF('day', TRY_TO_TIMESTAMP("order_purchase_timestamp"), TRY_TO_TIMESTAMP(NULLIF("order_delivered_customer_date", '')))), 2) AS avg_actual_delivery_days, ROUND(AVG(DATEDIFF('day', TRY_TO_TIMESTAMP("order_purchase_timestamp"), TRY_TO_TIMESTAMP("order_estimated_delivery_date"))), 2) AS avg_promised_days, ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN TRY_TO_TIMESTAMP(NULLIF("order_delivered_customer_date", '')) > TRY_TO_TIMESTAMP("order_estimated_delivery_date") THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS late_rate_pct FROM "BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE"."BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE"."OLIST_ORDERS" WHERE "order_status" = 'delivered' GROUP BY order_year, order_month HAVING COUNT(*) >= 500 ORDER BY order_year, order_month
